title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Recap for July 2, 2026, as US Markets Observe Independence Day Holiday

sections.title: Market Pulse: Holiday Closure and Last Session's Mixed Performance

items.headline: US Stock Markets Closed Today, July 4, 2026, for Independence Day (Observed)
items.summary: U.S. financial markets, including the NYSE and Nasdaq, are closed today, July 4, 2026, in observance of Independence Day. As the holiday falls on a Saturday this year, markets observed the holiday on Friday, July 3rd, and the last full trading session was July 2nd. Trading will resume on Monday, July 6, 2026. The prior session on July 2nd saw a notable divergence, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average reaching a new all-time high amidst a defensive rotation, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite declined due to weakness in semiconductor stocks. Overall market sentiment was influenced by a softer-than-expected June jobs report, which eased concerns about further Federal Reserve rate hikes.

items.statContext: Closed +1.14% (+594.83 pts) on July 2nd, marking a new all-time high.
Closed virtually flat (+0.01 pts) on July 2nd.
Closed -0.80% (-207.36 pts) on July 2nd, dragged by semiconductor weakness.

items.headline: Apple Leads Gains
items.summary: Shares of Apple jumped nearly 5%, providing strong support for the broader market.

items.headline: AstraZeneca Surges
items.summary: Pharmaceutical giant AstraZeneca saw its stock surge over 6%.

items.headline: ClearOne Soars
items.summary: Communications solutions provider ClearOne experienced a significant daily gain.

items.headline: Tesla Hammered
items.summary: Electric vehicle maker Tesla was down significantly amid market volatility.

items.headline: Micron Tech Plunges
items.summary: Despite strong earnings, Micron Technology's stock fell as semiconductor stocks faced pressure.

items.headline: Elicio Therapeutics Drops
items.summary: Elicio Therapeutics saw a substantial decline in its share price.

items.headline: Defensive Sectors Lead Amid Tech Rotation and AI Sell-off
items.summary: The market saw a significant rotation out of momentum-heavy technology and semiconductor names into defensive sectors. Health Care (+2.7%), Consumer Staples (+2.4%), and Utilities (+2.3%) posted the strongest gains, while Technology was the worst performer, down over 2.5%. The PHLX Semiconductor Index fell 5.4%. Analysts note this as a rotation rather than a recession signal, with investors locking in AI profits.

items.headline: Softer June Jobs Report Eases Fed Rate Hike Concerns
items.summary: The U.S. economy added a weaker-than-expected 57,000 jobs in June, well below the 110,000 to 130,000 anticipated. The unemployment rate edged down to 4.2%. This softer labor market data has prompted investors to scale back expectations for further Federal Reserve rate increases, with the probability of a September hike easing to 55% from 64.1%.

items.headline: FactSet Beats Expectations, Micron Secures Long-Term Contracts
items.summary: FactSet ($FDS) reported strong fiscal Q3 results, beating earnings estimates with $4.53 per share and a 6.4% rise in revenues, sending shares up nearly 9%. Micron Technology ($MU) announced blowout Q3 earnings and detailed Strategic Customer Agreements (SCAs) worth approximately $100 billion in contracted backlog through 2030, aiming for more predictable earnings and margin stability.

items.headline: Oil Prices Decline on Iran Deal Hopes; AI Infrastructure Spending Remains Key
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures fell nearly 2% to around $67 per barrel, buoyed by hopes of ongoing negotiations for a permanent end to the war with Iran. The significant investment in AI infrastructure continues to be a major market driver, though the sector experienced a recent 'crack' with profit-taking. Additionally, the U.S. plans to quadruple nuclear power capacity by 2050, indicating a long-term shift in energy strategy.
